---
title: "Full run — design"
subtitle: "Footprint, parameters, and measured zodiacal background"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
from pathlib import Path
import yaml
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
from astropy.table import Table
from astropy.coordinates import SkyCoord
import astropy.units as u

REPO = Path("../..").resolve()
CONFIG_PATH = REPO / "configs/full.yaml"
with CONFIG_PATH.open() as f:
    cfg = yaml.safe_load(f)
cone = cfg["pointings"]["region"]
```

## Science motivation

This configuration was built to support simulated **grism observations** of a
Wide-Field2 sub-region near **(RA = 10°, Dec = 0°)**, at four position angles
(PA = 0°, 10°, 170°, 180°). We need imaging coverage of the grism reference
position and a few arcminutes along the dispersion direction so that source
positions measured from imaging constrain the spectral trace extraction.

## Footprint filter

The pointings file (`pointings_full.ecsv`) is a subset of
`catalogs/HLWAS.sim.ecsv` (the full HLWAS-survey simulated pointings)
produced by `scripts/filter_pointings.py`:

```{python}
#| echo: false
print(f"region.type      = {cone['type']}")
print(f"region.ra        = {cone['ra']}°")
print(f"region.dec       = {cone['dec']}°")
print(f"region.radius    = {cone['radius_deg']}°")
print(f"bandpass         = {cfg['pointings']['bandpass']}")
print(f"only_pass        = {cfg['pointings']['only_pass']}")
print(f"only_segment     = {cfg['pointings']['only_segment']}")
print(f"only_visit       = {cfg['pointings']['only_visit']}")
```

A visit survives the filter if *any* of its dithers lands inside the cone —
matching visits contribute *all* their dithers (no partial inclusion). The
`only_pass/segment/visit` nulls here mean no further restriction after the
region test; the `smoke` config sets these to a single-visit subset.

The selection yields **6 visits × 3 dithers = 18 exposures** across passes 15
and 16, segments 490 and 521:

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "The 18 pointings selected by the full-run filter."
t = Table.read(REPO / "pointings_full.ecsv", format="ascii.ecsv")
df_p = t.to_pandas()
df_p[["PASS", "SEGMENT", "VISIT", "EXPOSURE", "RA", "DEC", "PA",
      "BANDPASS", "MA_TABLE_NUMBER", "EXPOSURE_TIME"]]
```

Passes 15 and 16 share the same (RA, Dec, PA) for every exposure — they are
**re-observations**, not distinct pointings. That matters for interpreting
per-pixel depth: the two passes stack at identical dither patterns, so depth
contributions from them are always paired.

## Pointings map (full HLWAS footprint)

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "The full HLWAS F158 footprint (grey dots = all ~67k pointings) with our target (red star) marked and the 0.5° filter cone drawn. The simulated survey sits south of Dec ≈ +1°, so our target at (10°, 0°) is near the northern edge — any roughly-isotropic filter cone drawn around it will necessarily have a lopsided pointing sample."
hlwas = Table.read(REPO / "catalogs/HLWAS.sim.ecsv", format="ascii.ecsv")
hlwas_f158 = hlwas[hlwas["BANDPASS"] == cfg["pointings"]["bandpass"]]
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
ax.scatter(hlwas_f158["RA"], hlwas_f158["DEC"], s=0.5, color="lightgray", alpha=0.5,
           label=f"{len(hlwas_f158):,} HLWAS F158 pointings")
ax.plot(10, 0, marker="*", color="tab:red", markersize=18,
        label="our target (10°, 0°)")
theta = np.linspace(0, 2*np.pi, 200)
ax.plot(10 + cone["radius_deg"]*np.cos(theta)/np.cos(0),
        0 + cone["radius_deg"]*np.sin(theta),
        ls="--", color="tab:red", lw=1, label="our 0.5° filter cone")
ax.set_xlabel("RA (deg)")
ax.set_ylabel("Dec (deg)")
ax.invert_xaxis()
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
ax.legend(loc="lower left", markerscale=3)
ax.set_title("HLWAS F158 — full simulated footprint")
plt.show()
```

The HLWAS F158 footprint as simulated runs from Dec ≈ −53° to Dec ≈ +3°,
with a hard upper edge around Dec ≈ +1° where the survey planning
truncates. **Our target at (10°, 0°) sits ~1° from that edge** — which is
why wide-area maps around it look asymmetric in Dec. It is not an artifact
of our filter.

## Pointings map (wide view)

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "HLWAS F158 pointings near our field. Grey dots are the full HLWAS F158 catalog in a 5° window around our target; red `+` are the 18 selected for the full run; the 0.5° filter cone is drawn as a dashed circle; the grism target (10°, 0°) is the red star."
hlwas = Table.read(REPO / "catalogs/HLWAS.sim.ecsv", format="ascii.ecsv")
hlwas_f158 = hlwas[hlwas["BANDPASS"] == cfg["pointings"]["bandpass"]]
c0 = SkyCoord(ra=cone["ra"]*u.deg, dec=cone["dec"]*u.deg)
c_all = SkyCoord(ra=np.asarray(hlwas_f158["RA"])*u.deg,
                 dec=np.asarray(hlwas_f158["DEC"])*u.deg)

def _draw_pointings(ax, radius_deg):
    near_mask = c0.separation(c_all).deg < radius_deg
    ax.scatter(hlwas_f158["RA"][near_mask], hlwas_f158["DEC"][near_mask],
               s=3, color="lightgray",
               label=f"{near_mask.sum()} HLWAS F158 pointings")
    ax.scatter(df_p["RA"], df_p["DEC"], s=80, color="tab:red", marker="+",
               lw=2, label=f"{len(df_p)} selected pointings")
    theta = np.linspace(0, 2*np.pi, 200)
    ax.plot(cone["ra"] + cone["radius_deg"]*np.cos(theta)
            / np.cos(np.deg2rad(cone["dec"])),
            cone["dec"] + cone["radius_deg"]*np.sin(theta),
            ls="--", color="k", lw=1, label=f"filter cone r={cone['radius_deg']}°")
    ax.plot(cone["ra"], cone["dec"], marker="*", color="tab:red",
            markersize=20, label="grism target")
    ax.set_xlabel("RA (deg)")
    ax.set_ylabel("Dec (deg)")
    ax.invert_xaxis()
    ax.set_aspect("equal")
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 8))
_draw_pointings(ax, radius_deg=5.0)
ax.legend(loc="lower right")
ax.set_title("Pointings — 5° around target")
plt.show()
```

Zooming in, the northern truncation from the full-footprint view above is
still visible as the thinning of grey points above Dec = 0°.

## Pointings map (close-up)

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Close-up around the 0.5° filter cone. The 18 selected pointings form three visit-clusters — one from segment 490 near (9.6°, −0.1°), and two from segment 521 near (10.5°, 0°) and (10.1°, +0.2°)."
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 8))
_draw_pointings(ax, radius_deg=0.8)
ax.set_xlim(cone["ra"] + 0.9, cone["ra"] - 0.9)  # invert x
ax.set_ylim(cone["dec"] - 0.7, cone["dec"] + 0.7)
ax.legend(loc="upper right")
ax.set_title("Pointings — close-up on the filter cone")
plt.show()
```

Each of the three visit-clusters covers its own ~1 WFI footprint; the gaps
between clusters are covered shallowly, which has consequences for per-skycell
coverage.

## Skycell coverage map

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Skycell centres from the full run, coloured by per-skycell median per-pixel depth. The design depth for this config is 6 (all 3 dithers of both passes); it is reached only in the cluster cores, not across the full filter cone."
summary = pd.read_csv(REPO / "output/full/compare/summary.csv")
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 8))
sc = ax.scatter(summary["center_ra"], summary["center_dec"],
                c=summary["median_depth"], cmap="viridis",
                vmin=2, vmax=8,
                s=35, edgecolor="k", lw=0.2)
ax.plot(cone["ra"], cone["dec"], marker="*", color="tab:red",
        markersize=20, label="grism target")
theta = np.linspace(0, 2*np.pi, 200)
ax.plot(cone["ra"] + cone["radius_deg"]*np.cos(theta)
        / np.cos(np.deg2rad(cone["dec"])),
        cone["dec"] + cone["radius_deg"]*np.sin(theta),
        ls="--", color="k", lw=1)
cb = plt.colorbar(sc, ax=ax, ticks=[2, 4, 6, 8])
cb.set_label("median per-pixel depth")
ax.set_xlabel("RA (deg)")
ax.set_ylabel("Dec (deg)")
ax.invert_xaxis()
ax.set_aspect("equal")
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
ax.legend(loc="upper right")
ax.set_title(f"{len(summary)} skycells, coloured by median depth")
plt.show()
```

## Run parameters

```{python}
#| echo: false
rows = [
    ("Tag",                 cfg["tag"]),
    ("Input catalog",       cfg["catalog"]["input"]),
    ("Input units",         cfg["catalog"]["input_units"]),
    ("Bandpass column",     cfg["catalog"]["bandpass_col"]),
    ("Pointings bandpass",  cfg["pointings"]["bandpass"]),
    ("Region type",         cone["type"]),
    ("Region centre",       f"({cone['ra']}°, {cone['dec']}°)"),
    ("Region radius",       f"{cone['radius_deg']}°"),
    ("Sim PSF type",        "stpsf (from 01_build_script.sh)"),
    ("MA table number",     int(t["MA_TABLE_NUMBER"][0])),
    ("Exposure time",       f"{float(t['EXPOSURE_TIME'][0]):.3f} s per exposure"),
    ("Sim date",            "2026-01-01T00:00:00"),
    ("N exposures",         f"{len(t)} × 18 SCAs = {len(t)*18} sims"),
    ("Design depth",        cfg["pointings"]["design_depth"]),
    ("Parallelism (xargs)", cfg["run"]["parallelism"]),
]
pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=["parameter", "value"])
```

## Measured zodiacal background

Per-bandpass expected zodi rates for Roman WFI are documented on the
[Roman WFI technical page](https://science.nasa.gov/mission/roman-space-telescope/wfi-technical/).
We quote the measurement below primarily in **MJy/sr** — that's the
unit a Pandeia ETC consumes as input and the unit romanisim injects
zodi in internally, and it's therefore the apples-to-apples unit for
comparison. The DN/s and e⁻/s columns are reported for reference; see
the [Discussion](discussion.qmd#zodiacal-background-in-mjysr-close-to-the-roman-reference)
note on the e⁻/s-convention mismatch between romanisim and Pandeia.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Sigma-clipped sky rate per SCA from one exposure. MJy/sr uses each SCA's `meta.photometry.conversion_megajanskys`; e⁻/s uses the median CRDS gain ≈ 1.87 e⁻/DN."
import roman_datamodels.datamodels as rdm
from astropy.stats import sigma_clipped_stats

GAIN_MEDIAN_EPERDN = 1.87
L2_GLOB = sorted((REPO / "output/cal").glob(
    "r0000101015521001006_0001_wfi*_f158_cal.asdf"))
rows = []
for p in L2_GLOB:
    m = rdm.open(p)
    data = np.asarray(m.data, dtype=np.float64)
    _, med, std = sigma_clipped_stats(data, sigma=3, maxiters=3)
    c_mjy = float(m.meta.photometry.conversion_megajanskys)
    del m
    sca = int(str(p.name).split("wfi")[1][:2])
    rows.append({"SCA": sca,
                 "sky_DN_per_s": med,
                 "sky_MJy_per_sr": med * c_mjy,
                 "sky_e_per_pix_per_s": med * GAIN_MEDIAN_EPERDN})
sky = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values("SCA")
sky.style.format({"sky_DN_per_s": "{:.4f}",
                  "sky_MJy_per_sr": "{:.3f}",
                  "sky_e_per_pix_per_s": "{:.3f}"})
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
mean_mjy = sky["sky_MJy_per_sr"].mean()
print(f"Mean across 18 SCAs: {mean_mjy:.3f} MJy/sr  "
      f"(range {sky['sky_MJy_per_sr'].min():.3f} – "
      f"{sky['sky_MJy_per_sr'].max():.3f})")

c_target = SkyCoord(ra=10*u.deg, dec=0*u.deg)
ecl = c_target.transform_to("barycentrictrueecliptic")
print(f"Field at (RA=10, Dec=0): ecliptic latitude {ecl.lat.deg:+.2f}°")

from astropy.coordinates import get_sun
from astropy.time import Time
sun = get_sun(Time("2026-01-01T00:00:00"))
elong = c_target.separation(sun).deg
print(f"Solar elongation on sim date: {elong:.1f}°")
```

A Pandeia ETC run for the same filter, coords and date gives an input
zodi of **0.38 MJy/sr**, so romanisim's sky is ~30% higher than
Pandeia's model for this field. Both codes place the field **on the
ecliptic** (latitude −4°) at a large solar elongation (~128°), which
is a structurally bright but well-observed part of the sky for a
Dec ≈ 0 target; within Roman's elongation constraint (~54°–126°), the
sim date is already near the upper end of the window, so shifting
dates cannot meaningfully lower the background.

## Data volume and wall time

Disk and wall-time cost of the full run. The pipeline is configured to
use `output/cal/` as a shared store (same filenames per visit/exposure/SCA),
so cal files carry over between smoke and full — only the novel L2s were
newly simulated for this run.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Stage outputs and storage. Counts and sizes from the live output tree."
import os
def du_mb(pat):
    total = 0
    for root, _, files in os.walk(REPO / pat):
        for f in files:
            total += os.path.getsize(os.path.join(root, f))
    return total / 1e9
def count(pat):
    return sum(1 for _ in (REPO / os.path.dirname(pat)).glob(os.path.basename(pat)))
rows = [
    ("L2 cal files (output/cal/*_cal.asdf)",         count("output/cal/*_cal.asdf"), f"{du_mb('output/cal'):.1f} GB"),
    ("L3 mosaics (output/full/mosaic/*_coadd.asdf)", count("output/full/mosaic/*_coadd.asdf"), f"{du_mb('output/full/mosaic'):.1f} GB"),
    ("  — of which coadd.asdf only",                  "", f"{sum(p.stat().st_size for p in (REPO/'output/full/mosaic').glob('*_coadd.asdf'))/1e9:.1f} GB"),
    ("L3 catalogs (output/full/catalog/)",            count("output/full/catalog/*_cat.parquet"), f"{du_mb('output/full/catalog'):.1f} GB"),
    ("  — of which cat.parquet only",                 "", f"{sum(p.stat().st_size for p in (REPO/'output/full/catalog').glob('*_cat.parquet'))/1e6:.0f} MB"),
    ("compare outputs (summary + 320 PNGs)",          "", f"{du_mb('output/full/compare'):.2f} GB"),
    ("per-sim + per-stage logs",                      "", f"{du_mb('output/logs')*1000:.0f} MB"),
    ("",                                              "", ""),
    ("CRDS cache (14 reftypes × 18 SCAs F158)",       "", f"{du_mb('crds_cache'):.1f} GB"),
    ("STPSF data",                                    "", f"{du_mb('stpsf-data')*1000:.0f} MB"),
    ("pixi env (default + report)",                   "", f"{du_mb('.pixi'):.1f} GB"),
]
pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=["artefact", "N", "size"])
```

Two small inefficiencies worth knowing about:

- `output/full/mosaic/` contains **duplicates** of the catalog outputs
  (`_cat.parquet`, `_segm.asdf`) that `MosaicPipeline` writes as a
  side-effect of its internal source-catalog substep; stage 05 then
  regenerates them into `output/full/catalog/`. The side-effect copies
  account for ~24 GB of the mosaic-dir total.
- Most of the L3-catalog volume is `_segm.asdf`; the actual source
  catalogs (`_cat.parquet`) are tiny (~74 MB for all 320).

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Wall-time per stage, 4-way parallelism on an m5a.2xlarge (8 vCPU → 4 physical cores, 32 GB RAM)."
wall = [
    ("Stage 02 — sims (romanisim)",      "~10 h",       "324 sims, 4-way xargs"),
    ("Stage 03 — asn (skycell_asn)",     "~1–2 min",    "320 asn JSONs"),
    ("Stage 04 — mosaic (MosaicPipeline)","4 h 5 min",   "320 coadds, 4-way xargs"),
    ("Stage 05 — catalog (SourceCatalog)","51 min",      "320 catalogs, 4-way"),
    ("Stage 06 — compare (per-skycell)", "12 min 30 s", "320 skycells, serial"),
    ("Total end-to-end (stages 02–06)",  "~15.5 h",     "≈ $5–6 at m5a.2xlarge on-demand"),
]
pd.DataFrame(wall, columns=["stage", "wall time", "notes"])
```

The EBS volume grew from 259 GB (initial) to 415 GB mid-run when the
mosaic step outgrew the original allocation. Current usage after the
full run is ~300 GB / 415 GB (~73%).
